sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.98 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$w$be a multilinear commutator word, and assume that$G$is a group where the set of$w$-values is covered by finitely many cyclic subgroups. Is it true that the verbal subgroup$w(G)$is finite-by-cyclic? This is true for lower central words (G.\,Cutolo, C.\,Nicotera, J. Algebra, { 324}, no.\,7 (2010), 1616--1624).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
